steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: After “gaseous,” the phrase consists of a noun “compounds” followed by “propylene and propane”—an appositive that specifies which compounds.

steps.title: Apply the restrictive appositive rule
steps.content:
Restrictive appositives are not set off by commas; non-restrictive appositives are.

Since “propylene and propane” specifies which compounds, it is restrictive and requires no commas.

Options A and D add unwanted commas; option C places a period mid-sentence.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
